name: Michail Dikaiopoulos
headline: Human-Centered AI researcher and software engineer
description: MSc candidate in Human-Centered AI with expertise in diffusion models and AI safety. Experienced software engineer with a focus on AI-driven solutions and backend development.

bio: MSc candidate in Human-Centered AI at the Technical University of Denmark (DTU), currently researching Riemannian Diffusion Models with a focus on safety considerations. Combines a strong academic background in AI and machine learning with three years of hands-on software engineering experience, including sole ownership of an AI product.

Passionate about bridging the gap between cutting-edge research and practical applications, particularly in challenging domains. Seeking research positions in the Netherlands to further contribute to the field of AI while tackling complex problems.

expertise.title: Diffusion Models
expertise.description: Expertise in developing and researching diffusion models, including Riemannian Diffusion Models with safety constraints.

expertise.title: AI Safety
expertise.description: Focus on incorporating safety constraints into AI models, particularly in generative and score-based frameworks.

expertise.title: Machine Learning
expertise.description: Advanced knowledge in deep learning, variational inference, Bayesian methods, and stochastic processes.

expertise.title: Software Engineering
expertise.description: Full-stack and backend development experience, including dynamic pricing systems and AI-driven solutions.

expertise.title: High-Performance Computing
expertise.description: Experience with GPU-accelerated simulations and high-performance computing techniques.

education.focus: Advanced Topics in Deep Learning, Advanced Machine Learning, Responsible AI and Explainability, Generative Models

education.institution: Athens University of Economics and Business

projects.title: (MSc Thesis) - Riemannian Diffusion Models with Safety Considerations
projects.description: Investigating methods for incorporating safety constraints into score-based generative models on Riemannian manifolds.
projects.detailedDescription: Implemented the RSGM framework (De Bortoli et al., NeurIPS 2022) with JAX using geodesic random walks and denoising score matching. Explored inference-time score modification techniques including barrier-augmented functions for avoiding interior obstacle regions.

projects.title: (BSc Thesis) - Forward-Looking Best-Response Multiplicative Weights Update for Zero-Sum Games
projects.description: Implemented and benchmarked the FLBR-MWU algorithm for finding Nash equilibria in bilinear zero-sum games.
projects.detailedDescription: Conducted extensive experiments with 20x20 game matrices across three payoff distributions and demonstrated 84x faster convergence compared to OMWU baseline using Python, NumPy, and Jupyter Notebook with $10^{6}$ iteration benchmarks.

projects.title: Lottery Ticket Hypothesis Study
projects.description: Reproduced and extended the Lottery Ticket Hypothesis across CNN, VGG, and BERT.
projects.detailedDescription: Implemented iterative magnitude pruning and few-shot transfer learning with ensemble sparse sub-networks in PyTorch.

projects.title: Patient Mortality Classification with Electronic Health Records
projects.description: Re-developed the EHRMamba model for the Physionet2012 dataset to predict early patient mortality.
projects.detailedDescription: Leveraged DTU GPU resources to reach 85% accuracy on early patient mortality prediction using PyTorch.

projects.title: Machine Learning Operations - Plant Leaf Health Classification
projects.description: Trained and deployed a plant leaf health model on Google Cloud.
projects.detailedDescription: Used Vertex AI, Cloud Run, FastAPI, Streamlit, Docker, and CI/CD with GitHub Actions for deployment.

projects.title: Multi-Agent AI System for Hospital Domain Coordination
projects.description: Implemented Conflict-Based Search (CBS) with A* for multi-agent pathfinding and coordination.
projects.detailedDescription: Developed a system for multi-agent coordination under time and action constraints.

projects.title: HPC Thermal Simulation
projects.description: GPU-accelerated heat diffusion simulation using CUDA, Numba, and CuPy.

workExperience.title: AI & Full-Stack Engineer - Leadership Advisor Group
workExperience.description: Developed an end-to-end solution for producing deliverable Board and C-Suite evaluation reports.
workExperience.detailedDescription: Sole owner of the solution for its entire lifecycle, producing high-level results in less than a year of development. Combined interface, backend, and an LLM prompting pipeline for automated report generation.

workExperience.title: Teaching Assistant - Technical University of Denmark
workExperience.description: Assisted in the teaching process of the MSc course Python and High-Performance Computing.

workExperience.title: Backend Software Engineer - Blueground
workExperience.description: Developed a dynamic pricing microservice and backend services in Kotlin.
workExperience.detailedDescription: Responsible for developing a dynamic pricing microservice that supported company's pricing operations and increased revenue. Collaborated with product and data teams to define pricing logic and requirements.
